ddls:
CREATE TABLE "communication_modes" (
  "mode_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each communication mode */,
  "mode_name" TEXT /* Name of the communication mode (e.g., IFF/ATC, NAVAIDS) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the communication mode */,
  "active_status" INTEGER /* Indicates if the communication mode is currently active */,
  "last_test_date" TEXT /* Date of the last test or maintenance check */,
  "test_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of required tests (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operational_parameters" (
  "param_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operational parameter */,
  "mode_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated communication mode */,
  "param_name" TEXT /* Name of the operational parameter */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value for the parameter */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the parameter */,
  "min_value" TEXT /* Minimum value for the parameter */,
  "max_value" TEXT /* Maximum value for the parameter */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the parameter */,
  "sensitivity_level" TEXT /* Sensitivity level of the parameter (e.g., public, confidential, secret) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("param_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operational_parameters_mode_id FOREIGN KEY ("mode_id") REFERENCES communication_modes ("mode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parameter_definitions" (
  "def_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter definition */,
  "param_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated operational parameter */,
  "definition" TEXT /* Text describing the definition of the parameter */,
  "example_value" TEXT /* Example value to illustrate the parameter's usage */,
  "references" TEXT /* References to documentation or standards */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("def_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parameter_definitions_param_id FOREIGN KEY ("param_id") REFERENCES operational_parameters ("param_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "communication_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "mode_id" INTEGER /* ID of the communication mode related to the change */,
  "param_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operational parameter that was changed */,
  "change_value" TEXT /* New value set for the parameter */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the change occurred */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "change_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the change */,
  "approved_by" TEXT /* ID of the user who approved the change (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_communication_logs_mode_id FOREIGN KEY ("mode_id") REFERENCES communication_modes ("mode_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_communication_logs_param_id FOREIGN KEY ("param_id") REFERENCES operational_parameters ("param_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_communication_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "fault_reports" (
  "fault_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each fault report */,
  "mode_id" INTEGER /* ID of the communication mode related to the fault */,
  "param_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operational parameter associated with the fault */,
  "fault_description" TEXT /* Description of the fault */,
  "reported_date" TEXT /* Date when the fault was reported */,
  "resolved_date" TEXT /* Date when the fault was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" TEXT /* ID of the user who resolved the fault */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes on how the fault was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("fault_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_fault_reports_mode_id FOREIGN KEY ("mode_id") REFERENCES communication_modes ("mode_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_fault_reports_param_id FOREIGN KEY ("param_id") REFERENCES operational_parameters ("param_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., operator, technician, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or unit the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the mode */,
  "mode_id" INTEGER /* ID of the communication mode being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when access was granted */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, modify) */,
  "access_duration" TEXT /* Duration of the access granted */,
  "approved_by" TEXT /* ID of the user who approved the access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_mode_id FOREIGN KEY ("mode_id") REFERENCES communication_modes ("mode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the status entry */,
  "mode_id" INTEGER /* ID of the communication mode */,
  "is_enabled" INTEGER /* Indicates if the mode is enabled */,
  "is_damaged" INTEGER /* Indicates if the mode is damaged */,
  "is_malfunctioning" INTEGER /* Indicates if the mode is malfunctioning */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Date when the status was last updated */,
  "updated_by" TEXT /* ID of the user who updated the status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_status_mode_id FOREIGN KEY ("mode_id") REFERENCES communication_modes ("mode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "response_capabilities" (
  "capability_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the response capability */,
  "param_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operational parameter */,
  "response_type" TEXT /* Type of response (e.g., alert, notification) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the response capability */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the response (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "auto_trigger" INTEGER /* Indicates if the response is automatically triggered */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("capability_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_response_capabilities_param_id FOREIGN KEY ("param_id") REFERENCES operational_parameters ("param_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "protocol_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each protocol version */,
  "protocol_name" TEXT /* Name of the protocol */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the protocol */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date when this version was released */,
  "compatibility" TEXT /* Compatibility with other protocols or systems */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Date of the last update to the protocol */,
  "deprecated" INTEGER /* Indicates if the protocol version is deprecated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who received the alert */,
  "mode_id" INTEGER /* ID of the communication mode related to the alert */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message describing the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was generated */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., info, warning, critical) */,
  "action_required" INTEGER /* Indicates if action is required */,
  "action_taken" TEXT /* Actions taken in response to the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_mode_id FOREIGN KEY ("mode_id") REFERENCES communication_modes ("mode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operational_logs" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operational event */,
  "mode_id" INTEGER /* ID of the communication mode related to the event */,
  "event_description" TEXT /* Description of the operational event */,
  "event_date" TEXT /* Date when the event occurred */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the operation */,
  "operation_type" TEXT /* Type of operation (e.g., start, stop, test) */,
  "operation_status" TEXT /* Status of the operation (e.g., success, failure) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operational_logs_mode_id FOREIGN KEY ("mode_id") REFERENCES communication_modes ("mode_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operational_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parameter_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the parameter status entry */,
  "param_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operational parameter */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the parameter is currently active */,
  "last_checked" TEXT /* Date when the parameter was last checked */,
  "next_check_date" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled check */,
  "check_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of checks (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parameter_status_param_id FOREIGN KEY ("param_id") REFERENCES operational_parameters ("param_id")
)
